page: Amazon Web Services p3dn.24xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8175M processor with 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores operating at 2.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR4 RAM running at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of memory per core. For acceleration, it bundles eight NVIDIA Volta V100 GPUs with a total of 256 GB VRAM. Local storage consists of an 1800 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance supports a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a distinct contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The instance exhibits weak single-core CPU performance, as demonstrated by its stress-ng single-core score of 1379.49 and weak single-threaded gzip compression. However, multi-core performance is strong, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 80918.99 and a PassMark CPU score of 55735.65. Memory bandwidth is also highly efficient, reaching 4496.8 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 217.49 GB/sec for uncached large blocks.

The integrated NVIDIA V100 GPUs deliver top-tier performance for machine learning workloads. LLM inference benchmarks show top-tier prompt processing speeds, reaching 14926.15 tokens/sec for a small 135M model, 1176.5 tokens/sec for a medium 7B model, and 144.96 tokens/sec for a large 70B model. Text generation speeds are also strong, achieving 15.78 tokens/sec on the 70B model. Additionally, the instance demonstrates strong throughput for Redis SET operations at 75.0 million operations per second and static web serving at 46.07 GB/sec.

Given the high density of GPU and memory resources, this instance class is optimized for large-scale parallel processing rather than single-threaded execution. The qualitative cost-efficiency is highest when fully utilizing the eight GPUs and high-speed network interface for distributed training. Tradeoffs include lower efficiency for workloads bound by single-core CPU performance. This server is suited for large language model inference, deep learning training, high-performance computing, and distributed database operations.
description: Amazon Web Services p3dn.24xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance featuring 96 vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 8175M processor, 768.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and eight NVIDIA Volta V100 GPUs with 256 GB of total VRAM. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 1800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a 100 Gbps network interface. While single-core CPU benchmarks are weak, the instance delivers strong multi-core CPU performance and top-tier LLM inference speeds, processing 144.96 tokens/sec for large 70B models. This high resource density makes it highly effective for parallel processing but less efficient for single-threaded tasks. It is designed for deep learning, large-scale machine learning inference, and high-performance computing workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services p3dn.24xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance featuring 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 768 GB DDR4 RAM, eight NVIDIA Volta V100 GPUs, and 1800 GB NVMe SSD storage for parallel computing workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services p3dn.24xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance with 96 vCPUs, 768 GB RAM, eight NVIDIA V100 GPUs, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A GPU-accelerated platform combining eight accelerators, massive memory bandwidth, and high-speed networking for parallelized machine learning workloads.
bullet_points: Eight NVIDIA Volta V100 GPUs with 256 GB total VRAM
96 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8175M and 768 GB DDR4 RAM
1800 GB local NVMe SSD storage and 100 Gbps network bandwidth
Top-tier LLM inference performance across small, medium, and large models
Strong multi-core CPU performance contrasted with weak single-core efficiency
Best suited for deep learning training, LLM inference, and parallel computing
